title: Dacrycarpus cumingii
intro: Dacrycarpus cumingii, also known as the Philippine umbrella tree, is a species of evergreen tree that belongs to the cypress family (Podocarpaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Philippines, for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dacrycarpus cumingii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared into decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to make herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures.
taxonomy: Dacrycarpus cumingii is classified as a species of coniferous tree within the family Podocarpaceae, commonly known as the yellow meranti. It is native to Southeast Asia, including countries such as the Philippines and Indonesia. The Podocarpaceae family is a group of conifers that are primarily found in tropical regions. This family also includes other notable species such as Podocarpus macrophyllus and Nageia nagi. Dacrycarpus cumingii is considered to be an endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.
